reason: Both worlds pass all rubric thresholds. No P0 or P1 defects. Average 3.56, minimum 3. No red flags from the failure taxonomy. Four enrichment suggestions are recommended for implementation but none constitute blocking defects.
p2Defects: P2-1: omega bypasses .isLt extraction in MeetFin L03 and L11 (no change needed — pattern is enforced where it matters in L12)
P2-2: Fin.val_injective and Fin.ext (lemma form) not disabled — allows one-shot of MeetFin L05 (no change needed — same insight taught either way)
P2-3: rfl closes FinNavigation L01, L03, L04 — handled by conclusion text explaining named lemmas are needed for variable n
enrichmentSuggestionsToImplement: 1. Generalize FinNavigation decomposition to variable n (Fin (n+1)) — the plan calls for it, MeetFin already generalizes in L11, and FinTuples will need the general form. Most important suggestion.
2. Name Fin.lastCases in FinNavigation boss conclusion — low effort, follows MeetFin's pattern of naming automation after teaching the manual technique.
3. Note decomposition as type equivalence (Fin (n+1) ≃ Option (Fin n)) in boss conclusion — low effort, foreshadows Fintype and CountingTypes worlds.
4. Teach obtain as lighter destructuring syntax in MeetFin — medium effort, real-Lean skill that transfers to subtypes and existentials.
notes: The playtest audit is thorough and well-calibrated. All three P2 defects have convincing mitigations. Boss integrity is verified for both worlds — all subskills are seeded before the boss, and integration moves are appropriate. The enrichment suggestions marked 'Consider' (concrete application earlier, succ injectivity standalone, Fin 2 ≃ Bool, castSucc order embedding, induction connection, decide contextualization) are reasonable but optional. The four 'Yes' suggestions should be addressed before authoring the next world (FinTuples), as suggestion #1 in particular affects downstream dependencies.
